titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Thomas Little Heath to Robert Chalmers, 19 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Little Heath

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Sir Thomas Little Heath (1861-1940), the secretary of the treasury, to the Under-Secretary Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938). The letter discusses the payment of the London and North Western Railway Company for the expense of seven party members that traveled with Augustine Birrell. The charge came to £15. 12s. 6d and Heath is directing Chalmers to the Foreign Office. After the Easter rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. Heath was a British civil servant, mathematician, classical scholar, historian of ancient Greek mathematics, translator, and mountaineer.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8610

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
TREASURY CHAMBER
Any reply to this letter should be addressed to - The Secretary, Treasury, Whitehall, London, S.W. and the following number quoted 12672/16 19 May 1916. Sir, I have laid before the Lords Commissioners
of His Majesty's Treasury your letter of the 12th
instant (7924), relative to a claim for Â£15. 12s. 6d
made by the London and North Western Railway Company
in respect of the fares of seven members of the
party which accompanied Mr. Birrell to Ireland on
the 26th ultimo. In reply I am to state that my Lords
understand that the arrangements for the journey were
made by the News Department of the Foreign Office,
which has defrayed the other expenses of the party,
and in the circumstances They would suggest that the
Railway Company's account should be transmitted to
the Foreign Office for settlement. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant T.L.Heath The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle (I) Reqt
For copies of letter
of 9th inst. from
Command Pay Office
& enclosure;
also of this T.L.
(II) Send copies to
F.Office
TLH
20.V.16
